message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Patients in the UK at risk of Covid-19 pneumonia, but not needing immediate hospital attention, are to be given pulse oximeters to identify a fall in oxygen saturation (SaO2 or SpO2) at home. A recent finding in Covid-19 pneumonia is a dominant reduction in ventilation to perfused alveoli (V
                  <jats:sub>A</jats:sub>
                  /Q). A mathematical model of gas exchange was used to predict the effect of shunt or reduced V
                  <jats:sub>A</jats:sub>
                  /Q on SaO2 stability inferred from the slope of the PIO2 vs SaO2 curve as it intersected the line representing ambient PIO2. A ±1 kPa variation in PIO2 predicted a 1.5% and 8% change in SpO2 with 15% shunt and 0.4 V
                  <jats:sub>A</jats:sub>
                  /Q respectively. As a consistency check, two patients with pre-existing lung disease and 12 hour continuous SpO2 monitoring breathing air had gas exchange impairment analysed in terms of shunt and reduced V
                  <jats:sub>A</jats:sub>
                  /Q. The patient with 16% shunt and normal V
                  <jats:sub>A</jats:sub>
                  /Q had a stable but reduced SpO2 (circa 93±1%) throughout the 12 hr period. The patient with a V
                  <jats:sub>A</jats:sub>
                  /Q reduced to 0.48 had SpO2 ranging from 75-95% during the same period. SpO2 monitoring in suspected covid-19 patients should focus on SpO2 varying >5% in 30 minutes. Such instability in at risk patients is not diagnostic of Covid-19 pneumonia but this may be suspected from a dominant reduction in V
                  <jats:sub>A</jats:sub>
                  /Q if episodic hypoxaemia has progressed from a stable SpO2.
                </jats:p>
title:
Oxygen saturation instability in suspected covid-19 patients; contrasting effects of reduced V
                  <sub>A</sub>
                  /Q and shunt

reference.article-title: Evaluation of a computer program for non-invasive determination of pulmonary shunt and ventilation–perfusion mismatch
Physiology and Predictors of Impaired Gas Exchange in Infants with Bronchopulmonary Dysplasia
reference.journal-title: Am J Resp Crit Care Med

reference.unstructured: Olszowka AJ , Wagner PD . Numerical analysis of gas exchange. In: West JB , editor. Pulmonary gas exchange. New York: Academic Press; 1980. p. 263–306.

Inada-Kim M , Chimel FP , Boniface MJ et al Validation of home oxygen saturations as a marker of clinical deterioration in patients with suspected COVID-19 doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.11.06.20225938
Vindrola-Padros C , Singh KE , Sidhu MS et al. Remote home monitoring (virtual wards) during the COVID-19 pandemic: a living systematic review doi: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.10.07.20208587
